learning_goals: Deepen FastAPI and REST API knowledge
user_profile.background_notes: Experienced Python developer and data scientist with 10+ years experience. Built the WellBegun note-taking application primarily with AI assistance (Claude Code). Strong in Python and data workflows, but unfamiliar with many of the web frameworks, frontend technologies, and architectural patterns chosen by the AI during development.

technologies.name: Python
technologies.description: General-purpose programming language used for the backend
technologies.project_usage: Backend API server, services layer, data models, database access

technologies.name: TypeScript
technologies.description: Typed superset of JavaScript for frontend development
technologies.project_usage: Frontend components, Svelte stores, API client functions, type definitions

technologies.name: FastAPI
technologies.description: Modern async Python web framework for building APIs
technologies.project_usage: REST API server with routers, dependency injection, CORS middleware, lifespan management

technologies.name: Svelte / SvelteKit
technologies.description: Compiler-based UI framework with full-stack capabilities
technologies.project_usage: Frontend UI framework for all components, pages, routing, and state management

technologies.name: SQLAlchemy
technologies.description: Python SQL toolkit and Object-Relational Mapper
technologies.project_usage: Database models (Collection, Category, KnowledgeTriple), queries, relationships, migrations

technologies.name: httpx
technologies.description: Async-capable HTTP client for Python
technologies.project_usage: Calling the Ollama LLM API for generation and streaming

technologies.name: Pydantic
technologies.description: Data validation using Python type annotations
technologies.project_usage: Request/response schemas for FastAPI endpoints (CollectionCreate, CollectionOut, etc.)

technologies.name: Client-Server Architecture
technologies.description: Separating frontend (browser) and backend (API server) as independent applications communicating over HTTP
technologies.project_usage: The Svelte frontend and FastAPI backend are separate applications; the frontend fetches data from the backend via HTTP API calls

technologies.name: CRUD Operations
technologies.description: The Create/Read/Update/Delete pattern as the foundation for data management APIs
technologies.project_usage: Every entity type (notes, projects, sources, actors, plans, collections) follows CRUD patterns through routers and services

technologies.name: ORM (Object-Relational Mapping)
technologies.description: Mapping between in-memory objects and database tables, abstracting SQL away
technologies.project_usage: SQLAlchemy models map Python classes to database tables; relationships are defined as object attributes

technologies.name: Layered Architecture
technologies.description: Organizing code into layers (routes, services, models, database) with clear responsibilities
technologies.project_usage: Backend follows routers -> services -> models -> database pattern; frontend follows components -> stores -> API clients pattern

technologies.name: Reactive UI Programming
technologies.description: UI automatically re-renders when underlying data changes, rather than manually updating the DOM
technologies.project_usage: Svelte's reactivity system automatically updates UI when stores or component state changes

technologies.name: Entity-Relationship Modeling
technologies.description: Designing data models with entities, attributes, and relationships between them
technologies.project_usage: The data model defines entities (Note, Project, Source, Actor, Plan, Collection) with typed relationships via tags, knowledge triples, and foreign keys

technologies.name: API Design
technologies.description: Designing endpoints, request/response contracts, HTTP methods, and status codes
technologies.project_usage: Each entity has a router with standardized endpoints (GET /items, POST /items, PUT /items/{id}, DELETE /items/{id})

technologies.name: Event-Driven UI
technologies.description: User interactions trigger events that flow through components and update state
technologies.project_usage: Svelte components use on:click, on:submit, dispatch() for parent-child communication; stores broadcast state changes

technologies.name: Graph Data Modeling
technologies.description: Representing knowledge as nodes and edges rather than flat tables
technologies.project_usage: KnowledgeTriple model stores subject-predicate-object triples; KnowledgeGraph component visualizes entity relationships

technologies.name: REST APIs
technologies.description: Architectural style for client-server communication over HTTP
technologies.project_usage: All communication between Svelte frontend and FastAPI backend follows REST conventions

technologies.name: Reactive Stores
technologies.description: State management pattern using observable/reactive data containers
technologies.project_usage: Svelte writable/derived stores for collections, workspaces, and other shared state

technologies.name: Knowledge Graphs
technologies.description: Data structure using semantic triples (subject-predicate-object) to represent relationships
technologies.project_usage: KnowledgeTriple model links entities (projects, activities, sources) with typed predicates

technologies.name: RAG
technologies.description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation - grounding LLM responses in retrieved context
technologies.project_usage: Assistant feature retrieves relevant entities and graph neighbors before sending to Ollama LLM
